summary: CI/CD config validation for the new Hostinger FTP GitHub Actions workflow. All 17 static validation checks pass: deploy.yml has valid YAML, no presshold reference, uses SamKirkland/FTP-Deploy-Action@v4.3.5 (repo + v4.3.5 tag verified reachable on github.com → HTTP 200), correct Hostinger host/port/server-dir, yarn build in frontend/ with frozen lockfile, actions/checkout@v4 + actions/setup-node@v4 with node 20 + yarn cache pointing to frontend/yarn.lock, all required secrets (FTP_USERNAME, FTP_PASSWORD, REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL) referenced and documented in workflows/README.md, frontend package.json build script confirmed as 'craco build', craco.config.js present, local-dir set to ./frontend/build/. No runtime/UI testing performed (explicitly out of scope).
critical_code_review_comments: deploy.yml is clean and uses pinned versions for all actions (good supply-chain hygiene).

CI=false is set on the build step to prevent CRA from treating warnings as errors — pragmatic but worth flagging: any new lint warning will silently ship to production. Consider running a separate `yarn lint` step that is allowed to fail informationally, or fix warnings upstream.
dangerous-clean-slate: false is correctly disabled — prevents accidental wipe of unrelated files on the shared host.
README only references the broken `presshold/sftp-deploy` in historical/explanatory context, not as a real action invocation — acceptable.

PyYAML parses the workflow's `on:` key as Python boolean True due to the YAML 1.1 on/off truthy quirk. GitHub Actions uses YAML 1.2 so this is harmless, but if you ever lint with strict tooling consider quoting the key as `"on":` or `'on':`.
Workflow has no notification on failure (e.g., Slack/email). For a production deploy pipeline, consider adding a failure notification step.
test_credentials: N/A — static config validation, no auth flows tested.
context_for_next_testing_agent: This iteration validated only the GitHub Actions workflow file at /app/.github/workflows/deploy.yml (static config). Live FTP deployment cannot be tested from this environment — final verification requires pushing to the `main` branch of the real GitHub repo with the three secrets (FTP_USERNAME, FTP_PASSWORD, REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL) configured. Backend/frontend runtime testing was explicitly out of scope per the review request and was not run.
rca of the issue: N/A — this was a forward-looking validation of a CI fix, not a bug RCA. The original bug (presshold/sftp-deploy@v1.2.1 no longer existing on the Actions Marketplace) is correctly mitigated by switching to SamKirkland/FTP-Deploy-Action@v4.3.5, an actively maintained action whose repo and tag both return HTTP 200 on github.com.
